skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
skill.summary: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential depend...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. All 10 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation about parallel agent dispatching methodology. Backtick patterns detected are markdown code fences, not shell execution. No cryptographic algorithms, network requests, or system reconnaissance present. Safe for marketplace publication.

risk_factor_evidence.explanation: Backtick characters are markdown code fences, not Ruby/shell backtick execution. No actual shell commands present.

content.user_title: Dispatch Parallel Agents for Faster Debugging
content.value_statement: When facing multiple independent test failures, investigating them sequentially wastes time. This skill teaches you to dispatch one AI agent per problem domain, solving multiple bugs in parallel.
actual_capabilities: Identify independent problem domains in failing tests
Create focused agent tasks with specific scope and goals
Dispatch multiple agents to work concurrently on different issues
Review and integrate parallel agent results without conflicts
Reduce debugging time by solving multiple problems simultaneously
limitations: Only works for independent problems without shared state
Requires upfront analysis to identify independent domains
Does not help with related failures that might share fixes
Cannot be used when full system context is needed

use_cases.title: Multiple Test File Failures
use_cases.description: When 3+ test files fail with different root causes after a refactor, dispatch separate agents to fix each file simultaneously.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers debugging complex test suites

use_cases.title: Independent Subsystem Bugs
use_cases.description: When different subsystems break independently (e.g., auth, caching, API), assign each to a dedicated agent for parallel investigation.

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Failures
use_cases.description: When multiple pipeline stages fail for unrelated reasons, parallelize the investigation across agents to get to green faster.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Parallel Dispatch
prompt_templates.prompt:
I have multiple independent test failures. Please analyze and dispatch appropriate agents to fix each one:

1. [Failure 1 - describe file and error]
2. [Failure 2 - describe file and error]
3. [Failure 3 - describe file and error]

These are independent problems. Return summaries of what each agent finds and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Agent Task
prompt_templates.prompt:
Fix the failing tests in [test-file-name.ts]:

1. [Test name 1] - [expected behavior vs actual]
2. [Test name 2] - [expected behavior vs actual]

This is a [timing issue / bug / race condition]. Your task:
1. Read and understand the test file
2. Identify root cause
3. Fix the issue
4. Return summary of what you found and changed.

prompt_templates.title: Results Integration
prompt_templates.prompt:
Three agents have completed fixes for:
- Agent 1: [file A] - [summary]
- Agent 2: [file B] - [summary]
- Agent 3: [file C] - [summary]

Please:
1. Check if any fixes conflict
2. Run the full test suite
3. Verify all changes work together
4. Report final status

prompt_templates.title: Parallel Investigation Request
prompt_templates.prompt:
I need to investigate [number] independent issues. For each one, create a focused agent task:

Issue 1: [problem description]
Issue 2: [problem description]
Issue 3: [problem description]

Dispatch all agents in parallel. Wait for all to complete, then help me integrate the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a parallel investigation workflow

output_examples.input: We have 6 test failures across 3 files after a major refactor
output_examples.output:
I'll identify independent problem domains:

1. agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
2. batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
3. tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

These are independent domains. Dispatching 3 agents in parallel to fix each file.

output_examples.input: Integrate the results from our 3 parallel agents
output_examples.output:
All 3 agents completed successfully:
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug
- Agent 3: Added wait for async execution

No conflicts detected. Full test suite is green. Time saved: 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially.

best_practices: Always verify problems are truly independent before dispatching parallel agents
Give each agent specific scope, clear goals, and constraints on what to change
Require agents to return structured summaries for easier integration
Run full test suite after all agents complete to verify no conflicts
anti_patterns: Dispatching parallel agents for related failures that might share fixes
Creating agent tasks too broad like 'fix all the tests' without specific scope
Not providing enough context for agents to understand the problem domain
Skipping the integration step - always verify fixes work together

faq.question: How do I know if problems are independent enough for parallel agents?
faq.answer: Problems are independent if fixing one does not affect the others. If fixing a bug in module A would also fix a bug in module B, they are related. Also check for shared state - if agents would edit the same files, they are not independent.

faq.question: What is the minimum number of failures to use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill becomes valuable with 2+ independent failures. With 3+ failures, the time savings are significant. For single failures, use standard sequential debugging.

faq.question: Can agents interfere with each other when working in parallel?
faq.answer: Yes, if they share state like editing the same files, using the same database, or modifying shared configuration. Always verify independence before dispatching.

faq.question: How do I structure a good agent task for parallel dispatch?
faq.answer: A good agent task needs: (1) Specific scope - one file or subsystem, (2) Clear goal - what tests should pass, (3) Constraints - what not to change, (4) Expected output - what to return.

faq.question: What should I do if agents return conflicting fixes?
faq.answer: Review both changes to understand the conflict. You may need to pick one fix, modify both to work together, or investigate further. Run the full test suite to verify the resolution.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this methodology works with any AI agent system including Claude Code, Codex, and other AI coding assistants. The pattern is about dispatching multiple agent tasks concurrently.
